content.claim: School maintenance authority for villages under 3,000 inhabitants is held by tankerületi centers (school district centers), not by municipal governments, eliminating village-level formal authority over school closure decisions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Describes institutional structure but does not establish causal impact on village school outcomes
Does not account for informal village-level influence on tankerület decision-making
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
